text.title: GCP Production Deployments Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the gcp methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Use GKE Autopilot for new clusters unless workloads require GPU, privileged containers, custom node configs or Windows nodes.
rule.rationale: Autopilot eliminates node-pool sizing, OS patching and kubelet tuning — 20-40 hours/month of toil.

rule.statement: Authenticate every external workload (GitHub Actions, GitLab, AWS, on-prem) via Workload Identity Federation and disable service-account key creation at org level.
rule.rationale: Service-account keys are the leading cause of GCP credential exposure; WIF maps OIDC directly to IAM principals.

rule.statement: Never assign primitive IAM roles (Owner, Editor, Viewer) to service accounts; always use predefined or custom roles scoped to the resource.
rule.rationale: Primitive roles span hundreds of permissions and are unauditable in any non-trivial breach review.

rule.statement: Use Private Service Connect (not VPC Peering) for instances without public IPs that need Google APIs, and add private DNS for googleapis.com and gcr.io.
rule.rationale: PSC scales to many projects without IP-range collisions; peering forces collision-free CIDR planning across the org.

rule.statement: Set Cloud Run min-instances to 0 for cost-sensitive services and 1+ for latency-sensitive ones; tune concurrency to 80-100 for typical web apps and 1-10 for CPU-heavy work.
rule.rationale: Cold starts (200ms-2s) are the dominant Cloud Run tail-latency factor; concurrency tuning bounds per-instance CPU contention.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
